_comment: Inline fix snippets per flag. Rendered in the Priority Action Plan section alongside each flagged issue. Tokens {ENV_ID}, {ENV_NAME}, {ORG_URL}, {ORG_HOST}, {TENANT_ID} are substituted per-environment at render time. Edit/extend to match your organization's preferred remediation procedures.

NO_SCHEDULED_BULK_DELETE.Why: AsyncOperationBase grows indefinitely without a recurring Bulk Delete job — the #1 source of DB bloat in Dataverse.
NO_SCHEDULED_BULK_DELETE.Fix: Create a recurring Bulk Delete Job. Entity = System Jobs; Status Reason in (Succeeded, Canceled, Failed); Created On older than 90 days. Schedule weekly outside business hours.
NO_SCHEDULED_BULK_DELETE.Snippet:
# UI-only. Open:
#   https://make.powerapps.com/environments/{ENV_ID}/settings/datamanagement
# Navigate: Data management -> Bulk record deletion -> New

OLD_COMPLETED_JOBS_NOT_CLEANED.Why: Completed async operations >90 days old are the largest cleanup opportunity in most tenants. They cascade-delete their WorkflowLog children.
OLD_COMPLETED_JOBS_NOT_CLEANED.Fix: Add a weekly Bulk Delete Job: Entity = System Jobs; Status Reason in (Succeeded, Canceled, Failed); Created On older than 90 days.

HIGH_SUSPENDED_JOBS.Why: Suspended system jobs signal a broken plugin or infinite-loop workflow. Each suspended job holds its payload in AsyncOperationBase until resolved.
HIGH_SUSPENDED_JOBS.Fix: Investigate root cause first: Settings -> System -> System Jobs (filter Status = Suspended). After fixing the offending customization, bulk-delete the suspended backlog.
HIGH_SUSPENDED_JOBS.Snippet:
# Query suspended jobs via Dataverse Web API:
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok" }
Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/asyncoperations?`$filter=statecode eq 2&`$select=name,operationtype,errorcode,message&`$top=50" -Headers $h

HIGH_FAILED_JOBS_30D.Why: A high failure rate in the last 30 days indicates active breakage — typically a failing plugin, flow, or data import.
HIGH_FAILED_JOBS_30D.Fix: Identify the top offenders first, fix the root cause, then add a recurring Bulk Delete job for Failed jobs >30d.
HIGH_FAILED_JOBS_30D.Snippet:
# Top 10 recent failed jobs by name:
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok"; Prefer = 'odata.include-annotations="*"' }
(Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/asyncoperations?`$filter=statuscode eq 31&`$select=name,message,errorcode,createdon&`$orderby=createdon desc&`$top=10" -Headers $h).value

PLUGIN_TRACE_LOGGING_ENABLED.Why: Plugin trace logging fills Log storage rapidly. Intended for short-lived debugging only — never leave on in Production.
PLUGIN_TRACE_LOGGING_ENABLED.Fix: Advanced Settings -> Administration -> System Settings -> Customization tab -> 'Enable logging to plug-in trace log' = Off. Then clear existing trace records.
PLUGIN_TRACE_LOGGING_ENABLED.Snippet:
# Disable via Dataverse Web API (organization.plugintracelogsetting = 0):
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok"; 'OData-Version'='4.0'; 'Content-Type'='application/json' }
$orgId = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/organizations?`$select=organizationid" -Headers $h).value[0].organizationid
Invoke-RestMethod -Method PATCH -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/organizations($orgId)" -Headers $h -Body '{"plugintracelogsetting": 0}'

AUDIT_RETENTION_SET_TO_FOREVER.Why: AuditBase grows unbounded when retention is 'Forever'. Storage cost compounds every month.
AUDIT_RETENTION_SET_TO_FOREVER.Fix: Set a retention period (e.g., 365 days) in global audit settings, then delete existing logs older than your new retention window.
AUDIT_RETENTION_SET_TO_FOREVER.Snippet:
# Set retention via Web API (retainauditforever=false, auditretentionperioddays=365):
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok"; 'OData-Version'='4.0'; 'Content-Type'='application/json' }
$orgId = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/organizations?`$select=organizationid" -Headers $h).value[0].organizationid
Invoke-RestMethod -Method PATCH -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/organizations($orgId)" -Headers $h -Body '{"retainauditforever": false, "auditretentionperioddays": 365}'

MAILBOX_SYNC_ERRORS.Why: A mailbox in error state stops syncing Exchange activity and also generates TraceLog (Alert) entries that silently accumulate.
MAILBOX_SYNC_ERRORS.Fix: Settings -> Email Configuration -> Mailboxes. Filter on Status Reason = Error. Re-test each mailbox and resolve the underlying credential / permission issue.
MAILBOX_SYNC_ERRORS.Snippet:
# List mailboxes in error via Web API:
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok" }
(Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/mailboxes?`$filter=statuscode eq 3&`$select=name,emailaddress,testemailconfigurationscheduled" -Headers $h).value

BROKEN_CONNECTION_REFERENCES.Why: A connection reference with no live connection breaks every flow and canvas app that binds to it — often silently until a user clicks.
BROKEN_CONNECTION_REFERENCES.Fix: Open each broken reference in the solution explorer. Either rebind to an existing connection or create a new one.
BROKEN_CONNECTION_REFERENCES.Snippet:
# List connection references and their binding state:
# Requires Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell
Get-AdminPowerAppConnection -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" | Where-Object { -not $_.Statuses -or $_.Statuses[0].status -ne 'Connected' } | Select-Object ConnectorName,DisplayName,Statuses

ACTIVE_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Why: When the owning user is disabled, every connection owned by that user expires — usually silently. Flow stops working without an error the business sees.
ACTIVE_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Fix: Reassign the flow to an active service account or owner group. Repeat for every affected flow.
ACTIVE_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Snippet:
# Reassign one flow to a new owner (replace OIDs):
# Requires Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell
Set-AdminFlowOwnerRole -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" `
  -FlowName "<flow-guid>" -PrincipalType User `
  -PrincipalObjectId "<new-owner-oid>" -RoleName Owner

CLOUD_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Why: Cloud flows owned by disabled users will break the moment their connections expire. This is active breakage-in-waiting.
CLOUD_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Fix: Reassign or co-own each flow to an active owner (preferably a service account or M365 group).
CLOUD_FLOWS_OWNED_BY_DISABLED_USERS.Snippet:
# Bulk list of flows owned by a disabled user:
Get-AdminFlow -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" |
  Where-Object { $_.CreatedBy.userId -eq '<disabled-user-oid>' } |
  Select-Object FlowName,DisplayName,Enabled,CreatedBy

ORPHANED_CANVAS_APPS.Why: Canvas apps whose owner has left the tenant cannot be updated and will eventually break when embedded connections expire.
ORPHANED_CANVAS_APPS.Fix: Reassign owner to a service account or delete if unused.

UNCERTIFIED_CUSTOM_CONNECTORS.Why: Custom connectors that haven't been certified bypass the normal review process. They often touch sensitive endpoints with no DLP enforcement.
UNCERTIFIED_CUSTOM_CONNECTORS.Fix: Review each connector's OpenAPI definition and target host. Retire, replace with a certified alternative, or submit for certification.

NO_DLP_POLICIES_CONFIGURED.Why: With zero DLP policies, any maker can combine any two connectors — including HTTP + SharePoint, a classic exfiltration pattern.
NO_DLP_POLICIES_CONFIGURED.Fix: Define at least one tenant-wide DLP policy. Start by classifying HTTP, Custom Connectors, and SQL as Business/Non-Business/Blocked.
NO_DLP_POLICIES_CONFIGURED.Snippet:
# Create a tenant-wide DLP policy that blocks HTTP in non-business:
New-DlpPolicy -DisplayName 'Tenant-wide default DLP' `
  -EnvironmentType 'AllEnvironments'
# Then classify connectors with Add-ConnectorToBusinessDataGroup / ...NonBusinessDataGroup / ...BlockedGroup

DLP_POLICY_MISSING_ON_PRODUCTION.Why: A production environment with no DLP policy can silently leak data through maker-built flows and apps.
DLP_POLICY_MISSING_ON_PRODUCTION.Fix: Add this environment to an existing All/AllExcept DLP policy, or create an environment-specific policy covering it.
DLP_POLICY_MISSING_ON_PRODUCTION.Snippet:
# Add the env to a specific-scope DLP policy:
Add-PowerAppsEnvironment -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" `
  -PolicyName "<dlp-policy-guid>"

PROD_ALLOWS_HTTP_CONNECTOR.Why: The generic HTTP connector can call any URL and bypass DLP classification for the target. On Production, this is a meaningful exfil path.
PROD_ALLOWS_HTTP_CONNECTOR.Fix: In the DLP policy covering this env, move the 'HTTP' connector to the Blocked group (or at minimum to the Non-Business group if blocking breaks existing workloads).

TENANT_ISOLATION_DISABLED.Why: Without tenant isolation, makers can create connections from your tenant's Power Platform to other tenants' services (and vice versa).
TENANT_ISOLATION_DISABLED.Fix: Enable tenant isolation in the Power Platform admin center; allowlist only the inbound/outbound tenants you genuinely need.

PRODUCTION_NOT_MANAGED_ENVIRONMENT.Why: Managed Environments unlock Weekly Digest, Solution Checker enforcement, sharing limits, and audit reporting — governance controls that don't exist outside it.
PRODUCTION_NOT_MANAGED_ENVIRONMENT.Fix: Enable Managed Environments for this production env. Requires Power Platform Premium or Microsoft 365 E3+ licenses for users in the env.

MANAGED_ENV_SOLUTION_CHECKER_OFF.Why: Without Solution Checker enforcement, known-bad patterns (insecure HTTP calls, missing error handling) ship to production unchecked.
MANAGED_ENV_SOLUTION_CHECKER_OFF.Fix: Enable Solution Checker enforcement on this Managed Environment.

MANAGED_ENV_WEEKLY_DIGEST_DISABLED.Why: The weekly digest is the primary forcing function for admins to notice drift. Off = drift is invisible until someone goes looking.
MANAGED_ENV_WEEKLY_DIGEST_DISABLED.Fix: Enable the weekly digest email on this Managed Environment.

SYSTEM_ADMIN_OVERASSIGNED.Why: Every System Administrator can read and modify every row. On Production this is a meaningful insider-risk and compliance issue.
SYSTEM_ADMIN_OVERASSIGNED.Fix: Review the list of sysadmin holders. Downgrade anyone who doesn't need tenant-wide admin; prefer scoped roles (System Customizer, env-specific security roles).
SYSTEM_ADMIN_OVERASSIGNED.Snippet:
# List active users holding sys admin in this env:
$tok = (Get-AzAccessToken -ResourceUrl "https://{ORG_HOST}").Token
$h   = @{ Authorization = "Bearer $tok" }
$sa  = (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/roles?`$filter=name eq 'System Administrator'&`$select=roleid" -Headers $h).value[0].roleid
(Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://{ORG_HOST}/api/data/v9.2/systemusers?`$filter=systemuserroles_association/any(r:r/roleid eq $sa) and isdisabled eq false&`$select=fullname,domainname" -Headers $h).value

NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_ENVIRONMENTS.Why: When anyone can spin up environments, DLP coverage breaks immediately (new envs bypass 'specific' scope policies).
NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_ENVIRONMENTS.Fix: Lock environment creation to admins only in the Power Platform admin center.
NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_ENVIRONMENTS.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> Settings -> Who can create production/sandbox = 'Only specific admins'

NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_TRIAL_ENVIRONMENTS.Why: Trial environments are often overlooked by governance scans yet can still hit Dataverse, Graph, and SharePoint with makers' credentials.
NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_TRIAL_ENVIRONMENTS.Fix: Lock trial environment creation to admins only.
NON_ADMIN_CAN_CREATE_TRIAL_ENVIRONMENTS.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> Settings -> Who can create trial environments

NO_CUSTOM_SECURITY_ROLES.Why: Relying solely on OOB roles forces sysadmins to over-assign. Every org benefits from at least one custom read-only / reporting role.
NO_CUSTOM_SECURITY_ROLES.Fix: Create a custom role mirroring your most common job function and assign it instead of sysadmin.
NO_CUSTOM_SECURITY_ROLES.Snippet: # UI-only. Navigate: Advanced Settings -> Security -> Security Roles -> New

FO_BATCH_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Why: F&O batch jobs in Error state stall processing chains. Downstream jobs wait, history tables grow, users see missing data.
FO_BATCH_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Fix: In F&O: System administration -> Inquiries -> Batch jobs (filter Status = Error). Fix the root error, then resume or cancel the job.
FO_BATCH_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Snippet: # UI-only. Open F&O: System administration -> Inquiries -> Batch jobs -> filter Status = Error

FO_MISSING_CLEANUP_JOBS.Why: Missing cleanup jobs let F&O history tables (BatchJobHistory, DIXF staging, SalesParmLine, etc.) grow indefinitely.
FO_MISSING_CLEANUP_JOBS.Fix: See the Storage Cleanup Recommendations section for a per-job menu path. Schedule each missing cleanup as a recurring batch (180-day retention is a good starting default).
FO_MISSING_CLEANUP_JOBS.Snippet: # UI-only. Each missing job has its own menu path in the Storage Cleanup Recommendations table below.

FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_NOT_ENABLED.Why: A disabled cleanup job is the same as a missing one — the history keeps growing.
FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_NOT_ENABLED.Fix: Re-enable the batch job. Status should transition to Waiting/Executing on the next schedule.
FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_NOT_ENABLED.Snippet: # UI-only. F&O -> System administration -> Inquiries -> Batch jobs -> find the job -> Functions -> Change status -> Waiting

FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Why: A cleanup job in Error usually fails the same way every run until the root cause is fixed, so history continues to grow.
FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Fix: Open the batch job, read the log, fix the underlying condition (permission, lock, bad parameter), then Reset and re-enable.
FO_CLEANUP_JOBS_IN_ERROR.Snippet: # UI-only. F&O -> Batch jobs -> select job -> View log to diagnose -> Functions -> Reset status

DUALWRITE_MAPS_IN_ERROR.Why: A DualWrite table map in Error stops replication for that table in one or both directions. Data drift between CE and F&O starts immediately.
DUALWRITE_MAPS_IN_ERROR.Fix: Open the DualWrite admin page for this env, select the failing map, review the initial-sync or running errors, and resume/retry once the root cause is addressed.
DUALWRITE_MAPS_IN_ERROR.Snippet: # UI-only. make.powerapps.com -> env -> Dual-write -> Table maps -> filter Status = Error

ENV_VARS_MISSING_VALUES.Why: An environment variable without a value falls back to its default; for prod flows that expect a real value this silently causes wrong behavior.
ENV_VARS_MISSING_VALUES.Fix: Solutions -> [target solution] -> open the environment variable -> set Current Value. Prefer adding the value through the solution layer, not the default layer.
ENV_VARS_MISSING_VALUES.Snippet:
# List env vars with no value set:
Get-AdminPowerAppEnvironmentVariable -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" |
  Where-Object { -not $_.CurrentValue -or $_.CurrentValue.Length -eq 0 } |
  Select-Object DisplayName,SchemaName,DefaultValue

NO_DUPLICATE_DETECTION_RULES.Why: Without detection rules, duplicate Accounts/Contacts/Leads accumulate silently — fatal for reporting, segmentation, and outreach.
NO_DUPLICATE_DETECTION_RULES.Fix: Create at least the default Account/Contact/Lead rules and publish them. Verify the recurring Dup Detection job is scheduled.
NO_DUPLICATE_DETECTION_RULES.Snippet: # UI-only. Advanced Settings -> Data Management -> Duplicate Detection Rules -> New

NO_RETENTION_POLICIES.Why: Retention policies are the managed path to delete data on schedule while respecting legal hold. Without them, the only option is Bulk Delete — which does not respect hold.
NO_RETENTION_POLICIES.Fix: Create a retention policy per sensitive table (Accounts, Cases, Contacts) with your org's retention period.
NO_RETENTION_POLICIES.Snippet: # UI-only. make.powerapps.com -> env -> Settings -> Retention policies -> New

NO_RECENT_USER_BACKUPS.Why: Manual backups are the fastest restore path for 'I deleted the wrong thing' scenarios. System backups exist but reset a much wider scope.
NO_RECENT_USER_BACKUPS.Fix: Take a manual backup before risky operations. Admins can also schedule one ahead of each deployment.
NO_RECENT_USER_BACKUPS.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> env -> Backups -> Create (enter a label)

NOT_IN_ENVIRONMENT_GROUP.Why: Environment Groups apply shared rules (DLP, sharing, solution checker) to every env inside. Without membership, this env drifts from tenant policy.
NOT_IN_ENVIRONMENT_GROUP.Fix: Add the env to the appropriate Environment Group in the Power Platform admin center.
NOT_IN_ENVIRONMENT_GROUP.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> Environment groups -> [group] -> Add environments

NO_DEDICATED_ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN.Why: An env with no dedicated Environment Admin role has no accountable owner. When something breaks, nobody is paged.
NO_DEDICATED_ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN.Fix: Assign the Environment Admin role (preferably to a security group, not an individual user) in the admin center.
NO_DEDICATED_ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> env -> Settings -> Users + permissions -> Environment admin

ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN_IS_USER_NOT_GROUP.Why: If the only admin leaves the company, the env becomes unowned. Groups survive people changes; individuals don't.
ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN_IS_USER_NOT_GROUP.Fix: Replace the individual Environment Admin assignment with an M365/AD security group.
ENVIRONMENT_ADMIN_IS_USER_NOT_GROUP.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> env -> Settings -> Users + permissions -> Environment admin

DEEP_BUSINESS_UNIT_NESTING.Why: Business unit trees deeper than 4-5 levels start to break the security model in non-obvious ways (team visibility, cascading) and slow every security-role query.
DEEP_BUSINESS_UNIT_NESTING.Fix: Flatten the tree. Prefer teams + field security profiles for segmentation instead of deep BU nesting.
DEEP_BUSINESS_UNIT_NESTING.Snippet: # UI-only. Advanced Settings -> Security -> Business Units

LIFECYCLE_OP_FAILED_RECENTLY.Why: A failed copy/restore/reset usually leaves the source or target in a transitional state. Next op can compound the problem.
LIFECYCLE_OP_FAILED_RECENTLY.Fix: Review the failure in the admin center, retry or cancel, and verify source/target health before scheduling the next lifecycle op.
LIFECYCLE_OP_FAILED_RECENTLY.Snippet: # UI-only. Admin center -> env -> History -> filter State = Failed

HIGH_UNMANAGED_SOLUTIONS.Why: A large unmanaged-solution footprint is a direct-customize-in-prod smell. ALM eventually becomes impossible to reason about.
HIGH_UNMANAGED_SOLUTIONS.Fix: Consolidate and export as managed. Target state: unmanaged solutions only in dev environments.
HIGH_UNMANAGED_SOLUTIONS.Snippet:
Get-CrmOrganization | Out-Null  # confirm connection
# UI-driven ALM: export each unmanaged -> import as managed via pipeline

CONNECTIONS_IN_ERROR_STATE.Why: A connection in error state stops every flow and canvas app that binds to it. Typically a credential/consent drift.
CONNECTIONS_IN_ERROR_STATE.Fix: Open each erroring connection and re-authenticate. Where the owner has left, reassign first.

ORPHANED_CONNECTIONS.Why: A connection whose owner has left the tenant is one credential rotation away from breaking whatever uses it.
ORPHANED_CONNECTIONS.Fix: Reassign each orphaned connection to an active service account or delete if nothing binds to it.
ORPHANED_CONNECTIONS.Snippet:
# Reassign a connection's owner (admin scope):
Set-AdminPowerAppConnectionRoleAssignment -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" `
  -ConnectorName "<connector-id>" -ConnectionName "<connection-guid>" `
  -RoleName CanEdit -PrincipalType User -PrincipalObjectId "<new-owner-oid>"

HIGH_FLOW_RUN_FAILURE_RATE.Why: A flow with sustained failure rate > threshold is almost certainly broken and actively eating API request entitlements for nothing.
HIGH_FLOW_RUN_FAILURE_RATE.Fix: Identify the worst offenders via the maker portal; fix, disable, or delete.
HIGH_FLOW_RUN_FAILURE_RATE.Snippet:
# Get flows and check their enabled state + ownership:
Get-AdminFlow -EnvironmentName "{ENV_ID}" |
  Select-Object DisplayName,FlowName,Enabled,CreatedBy

HIGH_UNRESOLVED_DUPLICATES.Why: Every detection job run re-reports the same unresolved dup pair. DuplicateRecordBase grows, reports stay broken, users lose trust.
HIGH_UNRESOLVED_DUPLICATES.Fix: Clear the backlog via Merge or Deactivate in Advanced Find. For large volumes, export the list, clean offline, and upsert.
HIGH_UNRESOLVED_DUPLICATES.Snippet: # UI-only. Advanced Find -> Look for = Duplicate Records -> triage by entity

HIGH_QUEUE_ITEM_BACKLOG.Why: A growing queue backlog usually means nobody's processing the queue anymore — either the assignee left or the routing rule is broken.
HIGH_QUEUE_ITEM_BACKLOG.Fix: Review the queue in the app, fix routing, and reassign stale items.
HIGH_QUEUE_ITEM_BACKLOG.Snippet: # UI-only. Open the affected queues via app navigation
